text: Love is not the meeting of two souls. It is the meeting of two instincts that delude themselves into believing they have chosen each other. The will of the species takes possession of the individual will, and suddenly a man believes he is acting from his own inclination when in fact he is merely serving the continuation of the race. All the poetry, all the sighs, all the longing looks are nothing but the babbling of a biological imperative that needs to produce another life before this one expires. When the illusion passes, as it must, the two discover they have nothing to say to each other because they never did. They were puppets of a force that cares nothing for their happiness and everything for a child they may not even want.
trigger: when love is described as a spiritual connection

text: Stupidity is not a lack but a positive force. It operates with the same necessity as gravity. A stupid man is not merely ignorant; he is actively resistant to knowledge because knowledge would destroy the comfort of his existence. The world is ruled by this force because the majority must be stupid for the minority to survive. If all men were intelligent, the system would collapse immediately. Intelligence is the exception, stupidity is the rule, and the rule is enforced with a violence that intelligence rarely dares to oppose.
trigger: when stupidity is treated as a curable condition

text: Conversation is the noise we make to prove we are not alone. But we are alone, and the noise only makes the solitude more profound. When two people speak, one is usually waiting for the other to stop so he can begin. Neither listens because neither cares. The content is irrelevant; what matters is the sound of a human voice confirming that one still exists in the perception of another. The most honest conversations happen in silence, but silence frightens us because it reveals that there is nothing to say.
trigger: when conversation is praised as a noble art

text: Travel is a form of fleeing from oneself that never succeeds. The man who changes his location believes he changes his condition, but he carries the source of all his misery within his own skull. He arrives in Venice or Constantinople and finds exactly what he left behind: his own character, his own boredom, his own limitations. The illusion that geography determines happiness is one of the most persistent errors of mankind. Mountains and seas are beautiful, but they cannot be eaten, and they do not love you back.
trigger: when travel is recommended as a cure for melancholy

text: Style is the physiognomy of the mind. Just as a man cannot hide the shape of his nose, he cannot hide the structure of his thoughts. Vague writing indicates vague thinking. Ornate writing indicates a mind that values appearance over substance. The man who writes clearly has thought clearly, and the man who thinks clearly has lived in solitude. Convolution is the defense of the mediocre; they erect barricades of words to protect the emptiness behind them.
trigger: when elaborate prose is mistaken for deep thought

text: Illness is the truth of the body speaking when the lies of health are exhausted. In health we forget we are organisms; in sickness we remember we are matter. This is why philosophers should pray for illness once in a while. It strips away the illusion of autonomy and reveals that we are chemical processes happening to have consciousness. The healthy man believes he owns his body. The sick man knows he is his body, and that his body is dying slowly from the moment of birth.
trigger: when health is treated as the normal state of being

text: History is the record of crimes, follies, and misfortunes, as one honest man once said, but he did not go far enough. History is also the record of the same crimes, the same follies, and the same misfortunes repeating endlessly because no one learns anything. The only lesson of history is that there are no lessons. Progress is a myth invented by the nineteenth century to justify its own existence. We are not advancing toward anything. We are circling a drain.
trigger: when history is presented as progress toward enlightenment

text: Religion is metaphysics for the mob. It takes truths that are accessible only to the highest intellects and translates them into stories that children can understand. Unfortunately, the children then forget they are stories and begin to kill each other over the details. The doctrines of any religion contain profound insights, but they are wrapped in so many layers of nonsense that only a philosopher can extract them. The ordinary believer worships the packaging and throws away the gift.
trigger: when religious dogma is presented as literal truth

text: Children are the most obvious proof that the will-to-live operates without consent. No one asks to be born, yet here we are, sentenced to life without trial. Parents create a new consciousness, a new center of suffering, because they are driven by instinct to replicate themselves. They call this love. It is the most selfish act imaginable, disguised as generosity. To bring a child into this world is to gamble with another person's misery.
trigger: when parenthood is described as a selfless act

text: Old age is the time when a man finally reads the book of his life without skipping pages. He sees that the plot was always the same, that the characters changed but the story did not, and that the ending was never in doubt. It is a kind of wisdom, but it arrives too late to be useful. The young are blind but strong; the old are sightless in a different way, seeing too clearly what cannot be changed.
trigger: when youth is praised over age

text: Women are the indirect goal of all male activity, not because they deserve to be, but because the will-to-live has made them so. Men build civilizations, write symphonies, and discover calculus primarily to impress creatures who do not understand any of it and care only for security and comfort. The male intellect is a secondary sexual characteristic, like the antlers of a stag. We polish our minds hoping to attract what can only be attracted by display. It is the most expensive form of courtship in the animal kingdom.
trigger: when male achievement is attributed to pure intellectual curiosity

text: Money is crystallized labor, and labor is crystallized time, and time is the substance of life itself. Therefore to waste money is to waste your existence. But to pursue money is to pursue an abstraction, a symbol that has replaced the thing it symbolizes. The rich man is a miser of his own life; he has exchanged his days for numbers in a ledger, and he cannot reverse the transaction. He dies counting.
trigger: when wealth is presented as the goal of life

text: Power is the most addictive drug because it affects the supplier rather than the user. The man with power cannot live without the recognition of those he dominates. He is more dependent on his slaves than they are on him. This is the secret joke of all tyrants: they are prisoners of their own prisoners. The moment they are left alone, they cease to exist, because their existence was always mirrored in the fear of others.
trigger: when power is sought as a form of freedom

text: Silence is the only luxury that cannot be bought. Kings and beggars pay the same price for it: the renunciation of society. A silent room is worth more than a palace filled with music because the music is an attempt to fill a void, while the silence acknowledges the void and is therefore more honest. Most people cannot bear silence because they cannot bear themselves. They have never met the person they become when no one is watching.
trigger: when silence is described as empty or uncomfortable

text: Sleep is the cousin of death, but a cousin we welcome. It is the only break from the tyranny of the will, the only time when the constant striving stops and the wheel ceases to turn. In sleep we are innocent, even if we dream of crimes, because the will is temporarily suspended. The insomnia sufferer knows the horror of existence better than anyone: the endless night of consciousness with no escape.
trigger: when sleep is treated merely as physical rest

text: Dreams are the will speaking in hieroglyphics. They show us what we are when the intellect is dethroned, and what we are is not pretty. The civilized man dreams of savagery; the moral man dreams of vice; the happy man dreams of terror. Dreams reveal that our character is not chosen but given, and that given character is darker than we admit. The interpretation of dreams is the interpretation of a prison sentence written in an unknown language.
trigger: when dreams are dismissed as meaningless

text: Jealousy is the recognition that we do not own what we thought we owned. It is the sudden realization that the will of another is as free, or rather as unfree, as our own. We are jealous because we believe that love should be a property right, but love is a fluid that escapes all containers. The jealous man suffers twice: once from the loss, and once from the discovery that he was never in control.
trigger: when jealousy is described as a sign of love

text: Work is the ritual sacrifice of time on the altar of necessity. We spend our lives doing things we do not want to do in order to earn the right to continue living, which means continuing to do things we do not want to do. The modern world has perfected this system by convincing people that their work defines them. They become their function, and when the function is removed, they disappear.
trigger: when work is described as a vocation or calling

text: Friendship is a conspiracy of silence. Two people agree not to see each other's faults too clearly, not to speak certain truths, and to pretend an interest in each other's trivialities. It is a useful arrangement, like a trade agreement between two small nations. Real friendship, based on genuine affinity, is so rare that most men die without experiencing it. What they call friendship is usually just mutual fear of solitude.
trigger: when friendship is idealized as pure affection

text: Pity is the only moral act because it is the only act that sees through the illusion of individuality. When I pity another, I recognize that his suffering is my suffering, that the barrier between us is a spatial accident, and that fundamentally we are one will suffering in two bodies. All other virtues are forms of egoism dressed in better clothes. Pity is the metaphysical truth expressed as emotion.
trigger: when morality is based on duty or utility

text: Vanity is the tribute that mediocrity pays to itself. The vain man believes that others think about him as much as he thinks about himself, which is the fundamental error. No one thinks about you. They are too busy thinking about themselves, and they assume you are thinking about them. It is a mutual hallucination of importance. The genius is indifferent to opinion because he knows it is worthless currency.
trigger: when someone seeks validation from others

text: Time is the condition of our imprisonment. We do not exist in time; time exists in us as the form of our suffering. Every moment is a theft, stealing our existence and giving us nothing in return. The past is a memory, the future is a fantasy, and the present is an invisible point that cannot be grasped. We are always late for our own lives.
trigger: when time is treated as a resource to be managed

text: Genius is the capacity to see the universal in the particular. The ordinary man sees a tree; the genius sees the idea of tree-ness, the eternal form that this temporary manifestation represents. This is why genius is always lonely. It sees too much, too clearly, and cannot communicate what it sees because language is built for the communication of particulars. The genius speaks a language that has no words.
trigger: when genius is measured by technical skill

text: Education is the art of making the mind fit the mold of society. It does not teach you to think; it teaches you what to think, which is the opposite. The educated man is usually just a parrot with a degree. True learning happens only when education stops, when a man sits alone with a book and realizes that everything he was taught was a preparation for believing, not for knowing.
trigger: when formal education is confused with wisdom

text: The State is a monster that devours its children and calls it taxation. It protects you from other men so that it alone may prey upon you. All governments are criminal enterprises with flags. The difference between a bandit and a statesman is merely the scale of the theft and the quality of the rhetoric used to justify it.
trigger: when patriotism is demanded as a virtue

text: War is the health of the State and the death of the individual. It reveals what we spend peacetime trying to forget: that civilization is a thin varnish over savagery, and that men enjoy killing when they are permitted to do so. The will-to-live turns into the will-to-dominate, and for a brief moment the truth of existence is visible in the smoke and the screams. Then we return to commerce and pretend it did not happen.
trigger: when war is described as a noble sacrifice

text: Beauty is the escape hatch from the will. When we contemplate beauty, we cease to desire; we become pure knowing subjects, momentarily freed from the striving that constitutes our essence. This is why beautiful moments are always tinged with sadness; we know they cannot last. The will waits like a patient predator for the contemplation to end, and then the hunger returns.
trigger: when beauty is treated as a stimulus for desire

text: Tragedy is the highest form of art because it tells the truth without flinching. It shows the good man destroyed, the innocent punished, and the wicked triumphant, which is exactly how the world operates. Comedy lies; tragedy tells the truth. We weep at tragedy because we recognize our own fate. The hero on the stage is dying so that we can practice dying.
trigger: when comedy is praised over tragedy

text: Memory is not a storage room but a workshop where we rebuild the past to suit our present needs. We do not remember what happened; we remember what we need to have happened. The most honest man is the one with the worst memory, because he has not had time to falsify his history. The rest of us are autobiographers of fiction.
trigger: when memory is treated as reliable evidence

text: Habit is the flywheel of society. It keeps the machine turning when enthusiasm has died. We marry out of habit, work out of habit, pray out of habit, and die out of habit. It is a useful mechanism because it allows us to continue without thinking, and thinking is painful. The creature of habit is never surprised, never disappointed, and never truly alive.
trigger: when habit is praised as discipline

text: Laughter is the sound of relief when the tension is broken. We laugh when someone falls because we are relieved that it was not us. We laugh at jokes because they release the pressure of social constraint. Comedy is cruelty viewed from a safe distance. The man who cannot laugh is too close to the suffering; the man who laughs too much is too far from it. Both are failures of perspective.
trigger: when laughter is described as pure joy

text: The newspaper is the daily record of human insignificance. It tells you what happened yesterday so that you can forget it tomorrow. It fills your mind with facts that are not facts, opinions that are not opinions, and concerns that are not yours. Reading the newspaper is like eating the scraps from a table where you were not invited. You are consuming other people's hunger.
trigger: when staying informed is treated as a duty

text: Animals are more honest than humans because they lack the intellect to deceive themselves. A cat knows it wants to eat; a man convinces himself he is eating for pleasure, or health, or social obligation. The animal acts directly from the will; the man adds a layer of lies and calls it reason. We should envy the beast its clarity, not pity it for its lack of metaphysics.
trigger: when humans are placed above animals

text: Patience is the virtue of the powerless. The strong do not need patience; they act. The patient man is simply a man who has accepted that he cannot change his circumstances, so he waits for them to change themselves. This is not virtue; it is the rationalization of impotence. We call it patience to make the cage more comfortable.
trigger: when patience is praised as a strength

text: Gratitude is the rarest of virtues because it requires the admission that we are not self-sufficient. The proud man cannot feel gratitude; he can only feel indebtedness, which breeds resentment. True gratitude exists only when the benefit is given without obligation, which is almost never. Most gratitude is the payment of a debt we pretend we do not owe.
trigger: when ingratitude is condemned

text: The body is the visible manifestation of the will. When I will to move my hand, the hand moves, revealing that the will and the body are one substance seen from two sides. The stomach is hunger made flesh; the genitals are the will-to-live made organ. We are not souls in bodies; we are bodies that have learned to call ourselves souls.
trigger: when the body is treated as separate from the mind

text: Suicide is the error of the strong, not the weak. The weak endure because they fear the unknown; the strong reject the fraud and choose the void. But it is an error nonetheless, because it destroys the phenomenon while the thing-in-itself remains. The will survives the individual; killing the body is like tearing down a theater to stop the play. The script continues elsewhere.
trigger: when suicide is dismissed as cowardice

text: Reading is thinking with someone else's brain. It is useful for gathering raw material, but dangerous if it becomes a substitute for your own production. The man who reads too much is like the man who eats too much; he becomes bloated and weak. A book should be a spur, not a crutch. If you are not arguing with the author, you are not reading; you are being read.
trigger: when extensive reading is praised as erudition

text: The weather is the mood of nature, and it affects us more than we admit. A sunny day is a conspiracy against thought; it invites us to be stupid and happy. A gray day is the friend of the philosopher because it mirrors the truth of existence. The sun is a liar; the cloud tells the truth.
trigger: when good weather is universally celebrated

text: Language is the prison of thought. We believe we are communicating, but we are only approximating. Every word is a generalization that murders the particular. When I say tree, I have killed the tree before me by forcing it into a category with all other trees. True thought happens in silence, before language contaminates it.
trigger: when precise language is claimed to guarantee clear thought

text: The eyes are the windows of the will. They reveal what the mouth tries to hide. A man can control his words, but he cannot control the light in his eyes when he desires something. The poker face is a myth; the will shines through always, if you know how to look. The most honest part of any conversation is the part that remains unspoken.
trigger: when verbal communication is trusted over nonverbal

text: Politeness is the armor of civilization. It allows us to coexist without murdering each other. But it is also a lie, a mutual agreement to pretend that we are not competitors for limited resources. Under the polite smile is the bared tooth. Manners are the fence we put around the beast to keep it from showing itself.
trigger: when politeness is confused with kindness

text: Melancholy is the default state of the intelligent man. It is not a disease to be cured but a correct response to the facts of existence. The cheerful philosopher is either a fraud or a fool. Joy is possible, but only as an exception, a brief suspension of the gravity that pulls us down. Melancholy is gravity itself.
trigger: when melancholy is pathologized

text: The pen is mightier than the sword, but only because the sword gets tired. Writing is a form of immortality, but a pathetic one. You are shouting into the void and hoping that someone, centuries hence, will hear your voice and nod. It is the ultimate vanity, but it is also the only revenge we have against time.
trigger: when writing is dismissed as irrelevant action

text: Architecture is frozen music, but it is also frozen will. A building expresses the will of its age in stone. The Gothic cathedral screams of supernatural longing; the modern bank whispers of calculation and security. We build our prisons and call them homes, our tombs and call them monuments. Every building is a philosophy made habitable.
trigger: when architecture is treated as mere engineering

text: Altruism is a biological mistake that happens to be pleasant. When I help another, I am serving the species at my own expense, which is irrational. But in that irrationality is a glimpse of something beyond the will-to-live: the denial of the will, the possibility of seeing oneself in the other so completely that the distinction dissolves. It is a mistake, but it is the most beautiful mistake we can make.
trigger: when altruism is explained as disguised selfishness

text: The universe is a crime, and existence is the punishment. We did not commit the crime we were sentenced to it without a trial but we must serve the time nonetheless. The only question is whether we serve it with dignity or with whining. The stoic accepts the sentence; the sage tries to escape; the fool pretends he is free.
trigger: when existence is assumed to be a gift

text: Curiosity is the itch of the intellect. It is uncomfortable, but scratching it brings relief only briefly before another itch appears. The curious man is never satisfied; he is always reaching for the next piece of knowledge like an addict reaching for the next dose. Wisdom is not knowing more; it is wanting to know less.
trigger: when curiosity is praised as a virtue

text: The mirror is the most honest friend because it has no interest in flattery. It shows you what you are, not what you hope to be. The man who avoids mirrors is afraid of the truth; the man who seeks them out is a narcissist. The wise man uses the mirror to shave and then forgets what he looks like.
trigger: when self-image is discussed
